similarity_name: Clinical concept set or ontology relatedness
definition: Represent each patient or encounter by diagnosis, symptom, medication, procedure, or SNOMED/UMLS concept sets. Use set overlap as a baseline, or concept embeddings/ontology relatedness when available, to audit whether EHR model performance depends on clinical-history overlap with training patients.
example_usage: python set_jaccard_similarity.py --train train_ehr.csv --eval eval_ehr.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --set-col concept_codes

citations.title: BEDS-Bench: Behavior of EHR-models under Distributional Shift--A Benchmark

evidence.summary: BEDS-Bench motivates evaluating EHR models under distributional shifts that affect clinical deployment.
The benchmark partitions EHR data into intentionally dissimilar subsets, including demographic slices.

citations.title: Biomedical Concept Relatedness - A large EHR-based benchmark

evidence.summary: The paper frames EHR concept relatedness across diagnoses, symptoms, and medications as distinct from exact string matching.
Concept pairs are sampled from EHR co-occurrence and mapped to SNOMED-CT IDs.
The benchmark evaluates embedding and fuzzy Jaccard similarity for biomedical concept relatedness.

limitations: Simple set overlap ignores code hierarchy, negation, temporality, and severity.
Concept coding practices vary across sites and over time.
Patient similarity can encode protected attributes or care-access bias.
quality_gates: Report vocabulary, code mapping, lookback window, and whether concepts are diagnosis-only or multimodal.
Use ontology rollups or embeddings when exact-code overlap is too sparse.
Audit temporal/site similarity separately to avoid hiding deployment shift inside concept overlap.
